The '''All Terrain Ion Cannon''' ('''AT-IC''') was an uncommon variation of the [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transport]] [[Walker/Legends|walker]] equipped with a massive [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]]. Although ion [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]] were rarely found aboard ground vehicles, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] converted a small number of its All Terrain Armored Transport [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] into mobile ion cannons. The Empire deployed the All Terrain Ion Cannon for the mobile defense of strategic locations.

==Characteristics==
Utilized by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the All Terrain Ion Cannon (AT-IC) was an uncommon variant of their standard [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transport]] [[Walker/Legends|walker]]. The All Terrain Ion Cannon resembled the standard All Terrain Armored Transport design, except half of its structural body space was dedicated to supporting a massive [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]].<ref name="CSWE" />

==History==
Ion [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]] required a considerable power output, so they were rarely found aboard small ground vehicles, with some exceptions. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Commerce Guild/Legends|Commerce Guild]] both employed vehicles equipped with light ion cannons as [[Anti-aircraft weaponry|antiaircraft weapons]]: the [[Self-Propelled Heavy Artillery]] walker and the [[OG-9 homing spider droid/Legends|OG-9 homing spider droid]], respectively. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later, at some point following the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the Galactic Empire, leading to that government's rule over [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the Empire converted a small number of its All Terrain Armored Transport walkers into mobile ion cannons, which became known as All Terrain Ion Cannon walkers.<ref name="NEGWT" /> The Empire would utilize the All Terrain Ion Cannons as mobile defense walkers for strategic Imperial locations.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|They were very complimentary. They said that the new characters and vehicles were all very '''Star Wars''-esque' and that they really appreciated the fact that we wanted to keep this going, but they just were not ready to pursue that kind of thing.|Kenner designer [[Mark Boudreaux]] reflects upon Lucasfilm's rejection of ''The Epic Continues''|<ref name="Tales of Phantom Toys">{{GalaxyCite|2|Galactic Bazaar|Galactic Bazaar: Tales of Phantom Toys}}</ref>}}
[[File:AT-IC_toy.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Kenner's original All Terrain Ion Cannon prototype]]
The All Terrain Ion Cannon was originally one of several vehicles planned for inclusion in [[Kenner Products|Kenner's]] [[1986]] ''[[The Epic Continues]]'' toy line, an initiative to continue the success of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' franchise toy line following the [[1983]] theatrical release of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. The Kenner design team developed a number of new vehicle model prototypes for ''The Epic Continues'' through a [[Wikipedia:Kitbashing|kitbashing]] process, which involved taking pieces of existing toy models and combining them to create an entirely new model. For the AT-IC prototype, the Kenner designers took a full-size AT-AT toy model and replaced a large portion of its aft section with a rear-mounted ion cannon,<ref name="Tales of Phantom Toys" /> calling it a "heavily armed A.T.A.T used exclusively for mobile defense of strategic Imperial locations."<ref name="The Epic Continues Presentation Binder">{{WebCite|url=http://theswca.com/images-speci/85LineExt/index.html|text=Star Wars Is Forever: 1985-86 Line Extension Presentation Binder|author=Lisa Stevens & Vic Wertz|work=The Star Wars Collectors Archive|archivedate=20101224195719}} See [http://theswca.com/index.php?action=disp_item&item_id=50976 Page 36 - Imperial AT-IC - All-Terrain Ion Cannon (Photo)]</ref> However, [[Lucasfilm]] rejected Kenner's proposal for ''The Epic Continues'', rendering the AT-IC outside the realm of ''Star Wars'' [[canon]].<ref name="Tales of Phantom Toys" />

Details of the AT-IC and the background story behind the failed ''The Epic Continues'' toy line were first revealed in the [[1995]] article [[Galactic Bazaar|''Tales of Phantom Toys'']] from [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 2|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 2]], written by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]].<ref name="Tales of Phantom Toys" /> The AT-IC first officially entered ''Star Wars'' canon through the [[2004]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]'', co-edited by Sansweet, which provided a brief mention of the AT-IC among other ground vehicles equipped with ion cannons.<ref name="NEGWT" />

At present, photographs of one of Kenner's original presentation binders for ''The Epic Continues'' are hosted on [http://theswca.com/images-speci/85LineExt/index.html The Star Wars Collectors Archive website], including a [http://theswca.com/index.php?action=disp_item&item_id=50976 picture] of the original All Terrain Ion Cannon prototype model. The website notes that the AT-IC prototype's ion cannon was recycled from Kenner's original [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] playset.<ref name="The Epic Continues Presentation Binder" /> However, despite mentions in ''Star Wars'' reference material, no image of the AT-IC was ever officially included in [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]].
